title: vulpia myuros var. tenella
intro: Vulpia myuros var. tenella, also known as creeping melilot or false melilot, is a perennial grass-like plant in the pea family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Vulpia myuros var. tenella include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
